title: Long-read sequencing QC
summary: Assess long-read run quality, read-length structure, and downstream readiness before polishing, isoform, or variant analysis.
test_prompts: Review these long-read sequencing QC outputs and decide whether the run is ready for polishing or variant calling.
Summarize the QC gates that matter most before starting downstream ONT or PacBio analysis.

title: Long-read basecalling
summary: Plan platform-aware long-read basecalling with explicit chemistry, accuracy, and demultiplexing assumptions.
test_prompts: Choose a basecalling strategy for a long-read run and explain what chemistry or platform details still matter.
Tell me how you would document a reproducible ONT basecalling stage before downstream analysis.

title: Long-read isoform analysis
summary: Analyze long-read transcript data for isoform discovery, classification, and downstream expression-ready summaries.
test_prompts: Plan a long-read isoform analysis workflow and explain what annotation context you need before interpreting novel isoforms.
Summarize the QC checkpoints that should gate transcript-level conclusions in an Iso-Seq project.

title: Long-read structural-variant calling
summary: Call structural variants from long reads with explicit breakpoint support, reference provenance, and assay-aware caveats.
test_prompts: Design a long-read structural-variant calling plan and explain what evidence you need before interpreting breakpoints.
Tell me how long-read SV analysis changes if the reference build or sample context is unclear.

title: Long-read consensus polishing
summary: Polish long-read contigs or draft assemblies with explicit rounds, support evidence, and validation checkpoints.
test_prompts: Plan a consensus-polishing stage for a long-read assembly and explain how you would validate the polished output.
Explain when Medaka-style polishing is appropriate and what would make the result untrustworthy.

title: Long-read sequencing workflow
summary: Coordinate long-read sequencing analysis from basecalling or QC through polishing, isoform analysis, or structural-variant discovery.
test_prompts: Outline an end-to-end long-read workflow and show how you would branch between polishing, isoform analysis, and SV calling.
Adapt a long-read workflow when the project starts from basecalled FASTQ rather than raw signal files.

title: Metagenomic taxonomic profiling
summary: Profile microbial community composition with explicit database, resolution, and confidence-threshold provenance.
test_prompts: Choose a metagenomic taxonomic profiling path and explain what database and resolution assumptions you need to lock down.
Summarize the QC and contamination checks that should gate microbiome profiling results.

title: Metagenomic abundance estimation
summary: Estimate feature or taxon abundance for metagenomic cohorts with explicit normalization and compositional-analysis caveats.
test_prompts: Plan a metagenomic abundance-estimation stage and explain what makes the output safe for downstream group comparisons.
Tell me how you would explain compositional caveats when reporting microbiome abundance tables.

title: Metagenomic AMR detection
summary: Detect antimicrobial-resistance features from metagenomic data with explicit database and support-evidence provenance.
test_prompts: Plan a metagenomic AMR detection analysis and explain what database and support thresholds you need before reporting hits.
Summarize the major failure modes that can make AMR calls misleading in mixed microbial samples.

title: Metagenomic strain tracking
summary: Track strain-level relatedness or persistence in metagenomic cohorts with explicit sample-comparability assumptions.
test_prompts: Design a strain-tracking analysis for longitudinal metagenomic samples and explain what evidence is needed before claiming persistence.
Tell me how you would avoid overstating strain-level findings when depth or classification quality is limited.

title: Metagenome assembly
summary: Assemble metagenomic reads into contigs with explicit resource, contamination, and downstream-use assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a metagenome-assembly stage and explain what QC metrics should gate interpretation of assembled contigs.
Summarize when metagenome assembly is a better entry point than direct taxonomic profiling.

title: Metagenomics workflow
summary: Coordinate metagenomics analysis from taxonomic profiling or assembly through abundance, AMR, and strain-level interpretation.
test_prompts: Outline a metagenomics workflow and show how you would branch between profiling, assembly, AMR, and strain-tracking tasks.
Adapt a metagenomics workflow for a cohort that only needs microbiome profiling and downstream abundance comparisons.

title: Proteomics QC
summary: Review proteomics assay quality, missingness, and replicate consistency before quantitative interpretation.
test_prompts: Interpret these proteomics QC outputs and decide whether the dataset is ready for differential abundance analysis.
Summarize the assay-level QC checks that matter most before drawing quantitative protein conclusions.

title: Peptide identification
summary: Identify peptides with explicit search-space, FDR, and modification assumptions that stay visible downstream.
test_prompts: Plan a peptide-identification stage and explain what search and FDR settings must be fixed before quantification.
Tell me how you would keep peptide-identification provenance visible for downstream protein-level analysis.

title: Proteomics quantification
summary: Quantify proteins with explicit normalization, rollup, and missing-value handling decisions.
test_prompts: Plan a proteomics quantification stage and explain what normalization or rollup choices matter most downstream.
Summarize how you would prepare protein-level abundance outputs for differential analysis.

title: Proteomics differential abundance
summary: Perform replicate-aware differential-abundance analysis for proteomics data with explicit normalization and missingness caveats.
test_prompts: Design a proteomics differential-abundance analysis and explain what QC and normalization assumptions must already be satisfied.
Tell me how you would explain the main risks when reporting differential protein findings from noisy or sparse data.

title: Affinity proteomics
summary: Analyze affinity-based proteomics panels with explicit assay-unit interpretation and platform-specific limitations.
test_prompts: Plan an affinity-proteomics analysis and explain how platform-specific units affect downstream interpretation.
Summarize what makes affinity-proteomics results different from mass-spectrometry proteomics in a reporting workflow.

title: Proteomics differential workflow
summary: Coordinate proteomics analysis from peptide identification or panel QC through quantification and differential abundance.
test_prompts: Outline a proteomics workflow and show how you would branch between classic mass-spec quantification and affinity-proteomics paths.
Adapt a proteomics workflow when the user only needs QC and differential abundance from an existing protein matrix.

title: Population structure analysis
summary: Characterize cohort structure and ancestry-related variation before association or comparative interpretation.
test_prompts: Plan a population-structure analysis and explain how it should gate downstream association testing.
Tell me how you would summarize ancestry-related structure without overstating discrete population labels.

title: Population-genetic association testing
summary: Run cohort-aware association testing with explicit phenotype, covariate, and ancestry-adjustment assumptions.
test_prompts: Design an association-testing workflow and explain what phenotype and covariate definitions must be validated first.
Summarize the main QC and confounding checks before trusting association results in a mixed-ancestry cohort.

title: GWAS to PRS translation
summary: Translate association summary statistics into polygenic-risk-score workflows with explicit ancestry and calibration caveats.
test_prompts: Plan a GWAS-to-PRS workflow and explain what ancestry and calibration assumptions must be explicit before reporting scores.
Tell me how you would explain the portability limits of a PRS across cohorts.

title: Population-genetic fine mapping
summary: Perform locus-level fine mapping with explicit LD-reference and credible-set assumptions.
test_prompts: Design a fine-mapping analysis and explain how you would validate that the LD reference is compatible with the discovery cohort.
Summarize what makes a credible set interpretable or misleading in a fine-mapping result.

title: Mendelian randomisation
summary: Run Mendelian-randomisation analyses with explicit instrument, harmonization, and sensitivity-analysis assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a Mendelian-randomisation analysis and explain what instrument-validity checks you would require before causal claims.
Tell me how you would summarize MR sensitivity analyses without overstating causality.

title: Archaic introgression analysis
summary: Detect and summarize archaic-introgressed segments with explicit reference-panel and ancestry-context assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan an archaic-introgression analysis and explain what references and cohort context are required before interpreting segments.
Summarize the main ways introgression inference can be confounded by ancestry structure or reference mismatch.

title: Phylogenetic tree inference
summary: Infer phylogenetic trees with explicit alignment, model, rooting, and branch-support assumptions.
test_prompts: Design a tree-inference analysis and explain what model, rooting, and support assumptions must be stated before interpretation.
Tell me how you would summarize uncertainty when a phylogenetic topology is only weakly supported.

title: Phylogenetic divergence dating
summary: Estimate divergence timing with explicit calibration, temporal-signal, and uncertainty assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a divergence-dating analysis and explain what calibrations or sampling-time information you need before reporting dates.
Summarize how you would communicate uncertainty in phylogenetic dating results.

title: Population-genetics association workflow
summary: Coordinate cohort structure, association, PRS, fine-mapping, and Mendelian-randomisation tasks in one population-genetics workflow.
test_prompts: Outline a population-genetics association workflow and show where you would stop for cohort structure, association, PRS, and fine-mapping QC gates.
Adapt a population-genetics workflow for a project that stops after association testing and does not continue to PRS or MR.

title: Phylogenomics workflow
summary: Coordinate phylogenetic tree inference, divergence dating, and related comparative-evolution interpretation with explicit uncertainty gates.
test_prompts: Outline a phylogenomics workflow and explain where topology support and dating uncertainty should stop downstream interpretation.
Adapt a phylogenomics workflow for a project that only needs a support-aware tree and not divergence dating.

title: Hi-C data IO
summary: Prepare Hi-C matrices and related assets for downstream structural analysis with explicit resolution and normalization provenance.
test_prompts: Plan a Hi-C data-preparation stage and explain what matrix, resolution, and normalization metadata must be preserved for downstream analysis.
Summarize the compatibility checks you would perform before handing Hi-C matrices to structural-analysis skills.

title: Hi-C compartment analysis
summary: Infer and compare chromatin compartments from Hi-C data with explicit sign, resolution, and normalization assumptions.
test_prompts: Design a Hi-C compartment-analysis stage and explain how you would keep sign and annotation choices traceable.
Tell me what matrix or resolution problems would make compartment calls too unstable to interpret.

title: Hi-C TAD detection
summary: Detect topologically associating domains from Hi-C matrices with explicit resolution and stability caveats.
test_prompts: Plan a TAD-detection analysis and explain what resolution and QC assumptions must be met before you trust domain boundaries.
Summarize how you would communicate uncertainty in TAD boundaries from sparse Hi-C data.

title: Hi-C loop calling
summary: Call chromatin loops from Hi-C matrices with explicit support-evidence, resolution, and sparsity assumptions.
test_prompts: Design a Hi-C loop-calling stage and explain what matrix properties must be satisfied before reporting loops.
Tell me how you would avoid overstating loop evidence when matrices are sparse or poorly replicated.

title: Hi-C differential contact analysis
summary: Compare Hi-C contact structure across groups with explicit resolution, normalization, and matched-sample assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a differential-contact analysis and explain what matrix-normalization and replicate checks must be completed first.
Summarize the main risks when comparing Hi-C contacts across conditions at the wrong resolution.

title: Hi-C visualization
summary: Render Hi-C matrices and structural results in a way that preserves resolution, normalization, and feature provenance.
test_prompts: Create a visualization plan for Hi-C matrices and explain what contextual metadata must stay visible in the figure output.
Tell me how you would present Hi-C structural features without implying more precision than the matrix supports.

title: Hi-C workflow
summary: Coordinate Hi-C data preparation, structural feature calling, visualization, and differential-contact interpretation.
test_prompts: Outline a Hi-C workflow and show how you would branch between compartment, TAD, loop, and differential-contact analyses.
Adapt a Hi-C workflow for a project that only needs matrix visualization and compartment-level interpretation.

title: Copy-number analysis
summary: Run copy-number analysis with explicit segmentation, baseline, and sample-context assumptions.
test_prompts: Design a copy-number analysis and explain what sample context and segmentation assumptions must be fixed before interpretation.
Tell me how you would communicate uncertainty in noisy copy-number profiles.

title: Copy-number visualization
summary: Visualize copy-number events while preserving segmentation, scale, and threshold provenance.
test_prompts: Create a copy-number visualization plan and explain what plotting choices could mislead interpretation if baselines are unclear.
Summarize how you would prepare a copy-number figure for review without hiding segmentation assumptions.

title: HLA typing
summary: Call and summarize HLA alleles with explicit assay, resolution, and ambiguity handling.
test_prompts: Plan an HLA-typing analysis and explain what sequencing and reporting assumptions must be fixed before summarizing alleles.
Tell me how you would communicate ambiguity and confidence in HLA-typing results.

title: CRISPR screen QC
summary: Review CRISPR-screen library representation, control behavior, and replicate stability before scoring hits.
test_prompts: Interpret these CRISPR-screen QC outputs and decide whether hit calling should proceed.
Summarize the QC gates that matter most before trusting a pooled CRISPR screen.

title: CRISPR library design
summary: Plan CRISPR library design with explicit modality, control, and sequence-constraint assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a CRISPR library design and explain what target, control, and sequence constraints must be fixed before synthesis.
Tell me how you would document library-design assumptions so downstream screen analysis stays reproducible.

title: CRISPR editing outcome analysis
summary: Analyze CRISPR editing outcomes with explicit amplicon, alignment, and modality-aware assumptions.
test_prompts: Design an editing-outcome analysis and explain what amplicon or reference information is required before interpreting the result.
Summarize how you would analyze CRISPR editing outcomes without overstating noisy or low-depth calls.

title: CRISPR screen hit calling
summary: Call CRISPR screen hits with explicit control, replicate, and scoring assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a CRISPR screen hit-calling stage and explain what controls and replicate checks must already be satisfied.
Tell me how you would explain the main failure modes that make a screen-hit list unreliable.

title: CRISPR gene-level scoring
summary: Aggregate guide-level evidence into gene-level screen scores with explicit model and concordance assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a gene-level scoring step for a CRISPR screen and explain how you would validate guide concordance before trusting gene scores.
Summarize how gene-level scoring differs from simple guide ranking in a pooled CRISPR screen.

title: CRISPR screen workflow
summary: Coordinate CRISPR screen design, QC, editing-outcome review, hit calling, and gene-level scoring.
test_prompts: Outline a CRISPR-screen workflow and show where you would stop for library, QC, editing-outcome, and hit-calling gates.
Adapt a CRISPR-screen workflow for a project that only needs QC and hit calling from an existing guide-count matrix.

title: Multi-omics data harmonization
summary: Harmonize identifiers, metadata, and modality-ready inputs before multi-omic integration or reporting.
test_prompts: Plan a multi-omics data-harmonization stage and explain what must align before integration can begin safely.
Summarize the main failure modes that appear when modalities are linked at the wrong unit of analysis.

title: Multi-omics integration
summary: Integrate multiple omics modalities with explicit shared-structure assumptions and modality-balance checks.
test_prompts: Plan a multi-omics integration analysis and explain how you would validate that the integration preserves rather than erases biology.
Tell me how you would explain the major risks when one modality dominates a joint multi-omic embedding.

title: Spatial transcriptomics preprocessing
summary: Prepare spatial-transcriptomics inputs with explicit coordinate, image, and spot-level QC provenance.
test_prompts: Plan a spatial-transcriptomics preprocessing stage and explain what image and coordinate assets must remain linked.
Summarize the QC checks that should gate downstream spatial interpretation.

title: Spatial multi-omics interpretation
summary: Interpret spatial-transcriptomics together with linked modalities while keeping resolution and image-context caveats explicit.
test_prompts: Plan a spatial multi-omics analysis and explain how you would prevent spot-level data from being over-interpreted as single-cell biology.
Tell me how you would keep image, coordinate, and auxiliary-omic context visible in a spatial co-analysis report.

title: Multi-omics integration workflow
summary: Coordinate data harmonization, multi-omic integration, and spatially linked interpretation across modalities.
test_prompts: Outline a multi-omics integration workflow and show where harmonization, integration, and spatial interpretation should be gated.
Adapt a multi-omics workflow for a project that links spatial transcriptomics to one additional modality but stops before formal joint modeling.
